Lean Body Mass Calculator for Perfusion Planning

Calculate LBM and compare indexed flow requirements using actual BSA versus lean BSA in CPB workflows.

Lean Body Mass LBM

Fat-free body mass calculator

Lean body mass can help contextualize metabolic demand in patients with high or low adipose mass. Compare actual BSA and lean BSA flow outputs for CI target discussions.

Boer/Hume LBM — kg

Flow comparison table (CI 1.0 – 3.0)

BSA actual vs BSA lean flow comparison

CI Flow (Actual BSA) Flow (Lean BSA)

Clinical Notes

• LBM definition: Body mass excluding adipose tissue.

• Perfusion context: Helps compare full-BSA indexed flow versus lean-mass indexed flow in obesity/extreme habitus.

• Interpretation: Use as decision support with SvO₂, lactate, perfusion pressure, and local protocol.

▶ Methodology & limitations

Boer formulas: Male LBM = 0.407×W + 0.267×H − 19.2; Female LBM = 0.252×W + 0.473×H − 48.3.

Hume formulas: Male LBM = 0.32810×W + 0.33929×H − 29.5336; Female LBM = 0.29569×W + 0.41813×H − 43.2933.

BSA comparison: Actual and lean BSA are computed from the selected BSA formula, then flow is CI × BSA.

Limitations: Adult estimation formulas; not a pediatric reference model and not a substitute for bedside perfusion monitoring.
